review_title: Paycom makes my small HR team better!
description: Outstanding - implementation was thorough and supported. The training we received set us up for success. And the system is easy to use!
author: Christopher D.
pros: Ease of implementation and use! The reporting feature is robust, and the Personnel Action Forms make employee changes so easy!
cons: The entire system works well for my needs. I haven't found a feature yet that I don't like. I haven't incorporated every Paycom feature yet, but everything is great so far.

description: Overall, we have been very pleased with the outcome of switching to Paycom and find that it has created an easier and more efficient workflow.
author: Jennifer R.
pros: The Paycom Specialist assigned to our account is always accessible and quick to respond any time we have a question or concern. The ease of processing payroll sometimes feels almost too simple and having a one stop shop for everything from payroll to recruiting to onboarding, provides a more seamless process in HR. Our associates also love the ability to navigate the tool on their phones through the Employee Self Service portal.
cons: Would love for more functionality in the accrual categories to define sub-categories of time within buckets like PTO to make tracking for reporting purposes based on state laws a bit easier to navigate.

description: I have been using Paycom for 10 years and have added almost every module. I often find issues that the average user would not recognize, however, are significant in impacting the company's compliance or finances. My experience often far outweighs that of my rep so I have had to spend a lot of time training them on things they should be helping me with. Overall, I like the platform. It eventually will do all the things that will benefit the Company. However, the process in getting there needs improvement. It seems Paycom rushes into developments and then jumps to the next one without completely finishing the first. Most specifically when it comes to how modules interact with each other. when there is a new development that impacts sections of an existing, we often find out the hard way if all things it touches are not reviewed. This has happened several times, resulting in very negative impacts.
author: Angie S.
pros: As an employee and for a basic user, the platform works well and is user-friendly. The integration of modules helps to streamline and improve efficiency in processes, where things are fully reviewed prior to implementation .
cons: As an advanced client user, Paycom does not meet expectations. Developments are not completely thought through before implementing and it creates issues that could be avoided if fully tested.

description: I was the lead for our implementation. It was alot of work, but new it would be great. It has been an amazing experience and would highly recommend to any employer to use. I love all the trainings offered and my rep is ALWAYS willing to make time for me.
author: Tammy M Rinehart T.
pros: It's easy to use and understand. The service is amazing and my rep always responds quickly and helps make sure I understand the full process behind what I am trying to accomplish.
cons: Nothing comes to mind. I promote Paycom every chance I get and have been very satisfied with the implementation and long-term care program. There is nothing I do not like about Paycom.

description: Very positive, we've been supported at every steps of the process. We're a year and half post implementation and still learning, but this has been a huge improvement for our organization.
author: Rose P.
pros: Customer support Ease of use Data - we have exponentially more information at our fingertips in comparison to our prior paper processes. This allows us to act using real-time information and be more strategic.
cons: Honestly, no major complaints here. If I'm being picky, I'd like to change the home page tiles and move them around to access my favorites quicker. Instead of i want up top, I'd rather jump to employee changes.

description: I have been in my role and using Paycom for about 9 months and feel like I have an overall good sense of how to utilize the key features. I am often overwhelmed by the amount of features and regularly unsure of what each function does. I think Paycom is capable of doing all of the tasks I want it too but I have no idea how to make changes to get to that point.
author: Jeanine C.
pros: I love having the ability to access most features from my phone. The team is also very responsive although sometimes don't always have a quick answer, it is pretty easy to get in touch with a representative.
cons: It is incredibly challenging to figure out where/how to make changes to certain things. Or seems to be multiple locations to make changes that are very similar. A good example is adding a pay code to a specific employee, often times we need to go to the employees profile to see what labor allocation profile they are in, then go to the labor allocation profiles and either create a new one or find an existing one that allows the correct access, then go back to the employees profile to change it to this and then also edit the user access profile for the manager to be able to see this employee. It is just a lot of steps for simple procedures. This is just one example but feel similar about other features.

review_title: 6 years in and still thrilled with the platform!
description: Stellar. Paycom is user friendly and maximizing the employee experience is critical to successful buy in. Customer support is unparalleled. Paycom consistently executes system improvements that improve internal process and maximize operational efforts.
author: Jynger M.
pros: I implemented 5 client codes simultaneously in 2020. The implementation teams' support was outstanding and unparalleled in comparison with experiences with competing platforms. Flash forward to 2026, and we are still impressed and happy with the system advances and continuous improvement. However, it is the level of support that is the key differentiator.
cons: The only consistent complaint I've had is that scheduled reports deliver to the client inbox inside Paycom, and cannot be emailed as an attachment outside the platform. I understand the security risk to PHI, but it would still be nice if used in alignment with user access inside the system.

review_title: Hit or Miss with Support, Easy Payroll. Take time to Implement
description: A mixed bag. The last 6 months or so with our new support team has definitely been great though. I wish it could have been this way all along. Overall though, I don’t know if I’d choose Paycom in a line up again. Implementation seemed to go great but you don’t really know what was missed until you’re in the system. Do not rush that process. And do the trainings. Make sure you know what you are doing before you go live, and check EARLY to make sure data was transferred correctly, and there aren’t accrual issues. They do offer a lot of support options, and again it’s easily accessible. So if this is important to you, you will get it with Paycom.
author: Jillian M.
pros: Paycom overall is a good system. When implemented correctly, and when you purchase all modules it functions decently well. Payroll is quite easy with th timekeeping module in place.
cons: your support team makes all the difference right along with who implements you. Our current support team is great. Before there were several issues and it rooted back to implementation. While support was always easy to get ahold of, there was a disconnect between myself and our assigned rep for awhile. They made it very challenging to get our rep reassigned. There seems to be challenges with having accrual limits stop at a set yearly amount when you also allow people to earn PTO when they work overtime. This is very strange to me but they never resolved this issue for us. I do not like the price per payroll set up. But it isn’t very much to run a payroll off cycle either.

description: Excellent. We have had fantastic customer service on the whole and it is a vital part of our organization.
author: Jessica S.
pros: The comprehensive customer support and the quarterly in person problem solving time we receive. 97% of the time I speak to a person and don't have to leave a message. They proactively reach out to us when there are changes in requirements.
cons: It has a lot of features for a small organization, but it can be hard to implement and keep up with improvements Paycom makes.

description: My overall experience with Paycom was very poor. Great promises, high hopes that things would be addressed, and never once was anything delivered on.
author: Katie W.
pros: The sales people were great!.... The employee mobile application was also enjoyed by my staff and something they felt was easy to navigate.
cons: Implementation and customer service were atrocious. I'm truly not sure I've ever had a worse experience with a vendor. They set things up improperly - we had spreadsheets sent to them with tax information that was never setup as it should have been - and we spent hours upon hours fixing things because of this. That's just one example of so many things that went wrong.

description: Overall my experience has been great. From what we were using previously to now, it has been a drastic change. We get much more interaction and support through Paycom.
author: Alixandria V.
pros: I like that it has streamlined my HR items and eliminated my need for paper. The Paycom staff are always very helpful and willing to assist when needed. Overall it has made my day-to-day operations much more efficient.
cons: The item that I like least about Paycom is scheduling. It does seem that the scheduling feature is more for a hospital setting or consistent schedules, which does not work for what we need.

review_title: Happy to be a part of the Paycom team!
description: Easy to use and I've been pleased with it, as long as positive updates keep happening. Our specialist knows our company in and out and while it's best working with him to reduce explanation, the rest of the team steps in well.
author: Julia B.
pros: It has a lot of on the go options that are helpful for managers out in the field. The customer service is always on point, we can resolve issues quickly.
cons: Lots of bugs when new updates are rolled out. This often gets in the way of normal processing and can add a lot of time to the website refreshing. When in registers, the inability to look up your full population of people is frustrating.

description: Having worked in several HCM environments, Paycom is easily the most well rounded and provide exceptional service.
author: Mike G.
pros: Far and away, Paycom provides the best customer service in the space. They ensure that the end user and administrators can efficiently and effectively perform tasks without assistance. Both the client and admin sides of the platform are intuitive and easy to learn, even for beginners. We are loyal Paycom customers and have no intention of even considering other platforms.
cons: While there are occasional hiccups, the support team is quick to reply and ensure resolution is swift.

description: Overall I love using Paycom and the technology that comes with it. It is simple, quick and easy to navigate, we love the reporting features and love the great customer service we receive.
author: Julianne D.
pros: I love the customer service we receive whenever we have questions or need assistance with a special project or report. I love that we have a local rep who checks in on us periodically. I love the ease of use and functionality of the software. It makes processing payroll easy and quick.
cons: I honestly do not have anything negative to say about Paycom. The Customer service is great, great reporting features and simple to navigate around.

description: On the outside, Paycom is a polished product and your team was able to sell it very well. Unfortunately, our implementation experience was chaotic. We have been disappointed by a number of functionalities Paycom offers, either because they did not deliver what was expected or because they are at times overly complex to use. Again, the value for money is not there. Overall, we regret to say that our experience has been frustrating and disappointing.
author: Laurent G.
pros: Paycom offers a comprehensive HR information system. Anyone working with Paycom is always very cordial.
cons: While comprehensive, Paycom HRIS is hard to navigate and use. As an organization, we have been very disappointed by the overall value for our money. Some of the modules we picked did not meet our expectations - the scheduling module for instance. Additionally, while all of our implementation managers and long-term support specialists have been really nice, the high turnover and the varying levels of expertise have adversely impacted our overall experience.

review_title: CRRs that are an extension of your Workforce
author: Valerie B.
pros: Paycom excels in customer support from the day-to-day support specialist to the customer relations rep who spends countless hours in person helping to make sure we utilize the system to the fullest extent.
cons: Paycom had some glitches with tax filing and adjustments for things like the 260E grants, and early on the PPP forms following Covid.

description: Many troubles with benefits and arrears, making our team have to do the labor that we are paying for within our agreement
author: Lindsey B.
pros: Customizable onboarding lists, ability to upload our company documents for ESS visibility, time off requests are simple, learning platform is nice.
cons: Too many updates that require you to sit in on webinars multiple times per day and week. No written communication about updates. Don't have time for these.

review_title: Paycom fighting to change the game.
description: Overall I think Paycom has a robust payroll module and data module along with some other great features that other HCM or HRIS and Payroll systems do not especially with the add in of IWant. There ATS has come along way and other modules keep improving. I think if you start small or mid size company and keep growing with Paycom it will be perfect. As for larger entities it may not be able to check all the boxes for now. I think Paycom is on the right track to keep changing the game.
author: Gabriel O.
pros: Love some of the simple setup of some things such as adding new locations and position management and a couple other things. Customer Support does do it best to try to resolve your issues. If an issue is hit usually can call a rep to get a ticket open with a pretty fast resolve. Also this is a very forgiving system and many things are correctable if someone make a mistake which is such a blessing. IWant has made it an ease of use for the employees and manager so I am thankful for that add on of AI function.
cons: Though the layout is good it could be great if there wasn't like 2 to 3 way to go to a module or section within the system. Having more then one way to get somewhere seems great but tends to confuse our managers and employees. Would like some improved functionality with effective dating and labor allocations.

description: Overall, I just love it! The system, the company and the support that me and my team gets is very valuable!
author: Krystyna N.
pros: Most I like is my representative, [sensitive content hidden], who is always available, very knowledgeable, very patient and kind. This makes my work so easy when I have a support from person like him! Second, it's how it looks (bright and colorful) and the way everything is set up, that is usually very easy to use. I also like that PayCom is adaptive and always ready to listen to our feedback and make update/changes based on our needs.
cons: Though I do really appreciate they Paycom constantly develops and improves everything in their system, I wish that new features were simply added to existing once versus very often the hole section can be changed so much that is hard to find something again especially if you do not use something very often by the time you need to use it again it can look completely different or in a very different spot and then you spend too much time trying to find something that could be done fast and easy.

description: One of Paycom’s biggest strengths is its employee‑driven approach. Employees are responsible for entering and updating their own information, which minimizes errors and cuts down on routine administrative tasks for HR. The payroll system is dependable, and real‑time data updates allow me to confidently process payroll and run reports without needing to reconcile information across multiple systems. Compliance tools and audit support are also helpful for ensuring accuracy and meeting regulatory requirements. From an administrative standpoint, Paycom’s reporting features are strong, and having a dedicated account representative makes issue resolution more efficient. Overall, Paycom is a solid solution for HR teams that prioritize accuracy, compliance, and process standardization. It works especially well in mid‑ to large‑sized organizations where employee self‑service and centralized data management can significantly reduce HR workload.
author: Carolyn G.
pros: Paycom has been a valuable all‑in‑one system for managing payroll, employee data, time and attendance, and overall HR processes. Having everything housed in a single platform significantly reduces duplicate entry and helps maintain data accuracy across departments.
cons: While the employee‑driven model reduces HR data entry, it also creates risk. Errors made by employees (missed punches, incorrect tax or benefit selections) often still require HR follow‑up and correction, shifting the burden rather than eliminating it.

description: Overall, it is easy to get a hold of a dedicated representative via email or phone; however, one bad representative can seriously impact the experience.
author: Josh H.
pros: I think that the value of Paycom supersedes the price that we pay. I do think that the functionality on the backend is helpful. In general, very easy to use.
cons: We have had some "wants" for a long time, such as automations that go outside of Paycom. There is not much that is available (e.g., 401k integration). We also had a few service issues where tickets were left open for nearly a full year, resulting in some serious tax implications. Not extremely customizable.

review_title: All In One Platform with Lots of Options
description: We transitioned to Paycom a few years ago and are still exploring what is possible for us to add on. There is always another option to add, with new tools coming out on a regular basis and updates to existing ones. I like the communication from the Paycom team on a regular basis and having a dedicated Paycom Specialist who is quick to respond to any questions we have.
author: Kelli C.
pros: I appreciate that it is an all in one platform for us. It's been great for managers and employees. We're now able to use it for employee recruitment, screening, onboarding, and support. It's cut down on the amount of time it takes to do things, which has freed up my time to focus on bigger projects.
cons: It's not always the most intuitive platform to find what you need. It takes some getting used to because there can be multiple ways to get to the same spot. I often need to reach out to our Paycom Specialist for help, which he is thankfully always willing to provide!

description: I have had a good experience with Paycom. It allows me to effectively manage my employee's schedules, attendance, punches and performance.
author: Lanoard B.
pros: Ease of use, easy edit features and mobile app option for employees and management. Payroll and performance management features.
cons: For my company it is used with Microsoft Edge. So, that is the only aspect that I do not like. I do not like the Microsoft Edge as a web browser.

review_title: Most beneficial for mid- to large-sized companies
description: I do not utilize all the numerous features as we are a relatively small company, from a staffing perspective. But the payroll processing and reporting functions are great.
author: Laurie O.
pros: The payroll features and options are numerous and very customizable. Payroll processing is simplified and proactive communication is appreciated.
cons: Too frequently our support representative has changed, providing little consistency and familiarity with how our company functions.

review_title: Fantastic option for a mid-size organization
description: Implementation is a breeze, and the system is user-intuitive. I struggle with the manual processes to overcome the lack of system functionality needed for an enterprise-level organization.
author: Josh M.
pros: Customer service is fantastic as long as the organization is enterprise-level. The user experience is amazing and easy to navigate.
cons: The lack of integration is painful. Paycom allows data to be sent but not received from third parties.
